\section{Enriched Adjoint Triangles}

For a complete, cocomplete and \hl{symmetric} monoidal \hl{closed} category $ \mathcal{V}$. 
Assume $ L: \mathbb{B} \longrightarrow  \mathbb{C}$ is a \hl{$ \mathcal{V}$-functor} and $ (L \vdash U,\eta,\epsilon)$ is a \hl{$ \mathcal{V}$-adjunction}.
We denote by 
\[
\chi: \mathbb{C}(L-,-) \cong \mathbb{B}(-,U-)
\] 
its associated \hl{ $ \mathcal{V}$-natural} isomorphism. 
So for every object $ X$ in $ \mathbb{B}$, and every object $ Z$ in $ \mathbb{C}$,
\[
\chi _{(X,Z)}= \mathbb{B}(\eta_X, UZ)\circ U _{LX,Z}
\] 

\begin{thm}\label{thm:enrichedadjointtriangle}
	Let $ (L \vdash U,\eta,\epsilon)$, $ (E \vdash R,\rho,\mu)$ be $ \mathcal{V}$-adjunctions such that 
	% https://q.uiver.app/#
	\[\begin{tikzcd}
		{\mathbb{A}} && {\mathbb{B}} \\
		& {\mathbb{C}}
		\arrow["J", from=1-1, to=1-3]
		\arrow[""{name=0, anchor=center, inner sep=0}, "E"', shift right=2, from=1-1, to=2-2]
		\arrow[""{name=1, anchor=center, inner sep=0}, "L", shift left=2, from=1-3, to=2-2]
		\arrow[""{name=2, anchor=center, inner sep=0}, "R"', shift right, from=2-2, to=1-1]
		\arrow[""{name=3, anchor=center, inner sep=0}, "U", shift left, from=2-2, to=1-3]
		\arrow["\dashv"{anchor=center, rotate=45}, draw=none, from=0, to=2]
		\arrow["\dashv"{anchor=center, rotate=135}, draw=none, from=1, to=3]
	\end{tikzcd}\]
	is a commutative diagram of $ \mathcal{V}$-functors. 
	Assume that, for each pair of objects $ (A \in \mathbb{A}, Y \in \mathbb{B})$, the induced diagram
	
	% https://q.uiver.app/#q=WzAsMyxbMCwwLCJcXG1hdGhiYntCfShKQSxZKSJdLFsxLDAsIlxcbWF0aGJie0N9KEVBLExZKSJdLFszLDAsIlxcbWF0aGJie0N9KEVBLExVTFkpIl0sWzAsMSwiTF97SkEsWX0iXSxbMSwyLCJMX3tKQSxVTFl9XFxjaXJjIFxcY2hpX3soSkEsTFkpfSIsMCx7Im9mZnNldCI6LTJ9XSxbMSwyLCJcXG1hdGhiYntDfShFQSxMKFxcZXRhX1kpKSIsMix7Im9mZnNldCI6Mn1dXQ==
	\[\begin{tikzcd}
		{\mathbb{B}(JA,Y)} & {\mathbb{C}(EA,LY)} && {\mathbb{C}(EA,LULY)}
		\arrow["{L_{JA,Y}}", from=1-1, to=1-2]
		\arrow["{L_{JA,ULY}\circ \chi_{(JA,LY)}}", shift left=2, from=1-2, to=1-4]
		\arrow["{\mathbb{C}(EA,L(\eta_Y))}"', shift right=2, from=1-2, to=1-4]
	\end{tikzcd}\]
	is an equaliser in $ \mathcal{V}$.
	The $ \mathcal{V}$-functor $ J$ has a right $ \mathcal{V}$ adjoint $ G$ if and only if, for each object $ Y$ of $ \mathbb{B}$, the $ \mathcal{V}$-equaliser of 
	% https://q.uiver.app/#q=WzAsMixbMCwwLCJSTFkiXSxbMywwLCJSTFVMWSJdLFswLDEsIlJMKFUoXFxtdV97TFl9KVxcZXRhX3tKUkxZfSlcXHJob197UkxZfSIsMCx7Im9mZnNldCI6LTJ9XSxbMCwxLCJSTChcXGV0YV9ZKSIsMix7Im9mZnNldCI6Mn1dXQ==
	\[\begin{tikzcd}
		RLY &&& RLULY
		\arrow["{RL(U(\mu_{LY})\eta_{JRLY})\rho_{RLY}}", shift left=2, from=1-1, to=1-4]
		\arrow["{RL(\eta_Y)}"', shift right=2, from=1-1, to=1-4]
	\end{tikzcd}\]	
	exists in the $ \mathcal{V}$-category $ \mathbb{A}$.
	In this case, this equaliser gives the value of $ GY$.
	
\end{thm}

\begin{proof}
	Suppose $J$ has a right adjoint $ G$.
	Then take any pair of objects $ (A\in \mathbb{A}, Y \in \mathbb{B})$.
	We have the induced diagram 
	\[\begin{tikzcd}
		{\mathbb{B}(JA,Y)} & {\mathbb{C}(EA,LY)} && {\mathbb{C}(EA,LULY)}
		\arrow["{L_{JA,Y}}", from=1-1, to=1-2]
		\arrow["{L_{JA,ULY}\circ \chi_{(JA,LY)}}", shift left=2, from=1-2, to=1-4]
		\arrow["{\mathbb{C}(EA,L(\eta_Y))}"', shift right=2, from=1-2, to=1-4]
	\end{tikzcd}\]
	which is an equaliser in $ \mathcal{V}$.
	Then under the associated $ \mathcal{V}$-natural isomorphism $ \chi: \mathbb{C}(E-,-) \cong \mathbb{A}(-,R-)$, we have:
	% https://q.uiver.app/#
	\[\begin{tikzcd}
		{\mathbb{B}(JA,Y)} & {\mathbb{C}(EA,LY)} &&& {\mathbb{C}(EA,LULY)} \\
		\\
		{\mathbb{B}(JA,Y)} & {\mathbb{A}(A,RLY)} &&& {\mathbb{A}(A,RLULY)}
		\arrow["{L_{JA,Y}}", from=1-1, to=1-2]
		\arrow[shift left, no head, from=1-1, to=3-1]
		\arrow[no head, from=1-1, to=3-1]
		\arrow["{L_{JA,ULY}\circ \chi_{(JA,LY)}}", shift left, from=1-2, to=1-5]
		\arrow["{\mathbb{C}(EA,L(\eta_Y))}"', shift right, from=1-2, to=1-5]
		\arrow["{\chi_{(A,LY)}}", from=1-2, to=3-2]
		\arrow["{\chi_{(A,LULY)}}", from=1-5, to=3-5]
		\arrow[from=3-1, to=3-2]
		\arrow["{\mathbb{A}(A, r_Y)}", shift left, from=3-2, to=3-5]
		\arrow["{\mathbb{A}(A,q_Y)}"', shift right, from=3-2, to=3-5]
	\end{tikzcd}\]
	in which $ q_Y= RL(\eta_Y)$ and $ r_Y = $
	
\end{proof}
